RollingHook.subtext: Many patients come to us after trying other treatments without lasting relief.
RollingHook.secondaryText: Our proprietary herbal formulation has been used in our clinics for decades and later inspired the PainFix topical pain relief range, now registered with Malaysia's Ministry of Health (KKM). Its anti-inflammatory properties were studied through laboratory research conducted with Monash University.

Differentiation.title: Built from Real Injuries — Not Just Theory
Differentiation.description: Shaolin medicine developed through treating <link>sprains, tears, and chronic pain</link> in real people. That practical tradition continues at YAPCHANKOR today.
Differentiation.transition: Rather than relying on one method alone, our treatment combines:

WhyChooseUs.title: Why Patients Choose Us
WhyChooseUs.subtitle: Not Just Physio. Not Just Chiropractic. Not Just TCM.
WhyChooseUs.description:
Most clinics rely on a single method when treating conditions like back pain, knee pain or shoulder injuries.

At YAPCHANKOR, treatment combines multiple approaches shaped by Shaolin injury medicine and refined through decades of modern clinical practice.

ScientificProof.title: Scientifically Studied. Clinically Proven.
ScientificProof.description: Our proprietary herbal formulation has been used in clinical practice for decades and later inspired the PainFix topical pain relief range.
ScientificProof.monashStudy: Laboratory research conducted with Monash University studied its anti-inflammatory properties at the cellular level.
ScientificProof.finding2: Consistent anti-inflammatory action across all stages tested
ScientificProof.finding3: Non-toxic with over 90% cell viability confirmed
ScientificProof.graphicCaption: Laboratory research conducted with Monash University

Compatibility.title: Works Alongside Modern Medical Care
Compatibility.description: YAPCHANKOR treatment is often used alongside physiotherapy, medical treatment, and <link>post-surgical rehabilitation</link>. Our role is not to replace other care, but to support recovery through an integrated approach.
Compatibility.support: Modern clinical guidelines recommend starting with <link>conservative, non-invasive treatment</link> before considering injections or surgery. At YAPCHANKOR, we combine physiotherapy with our Shaolin-based herbal formulation to support recovery in a structured and practical way.

ClinicalResults.title: Results in Real Patients
ClinicalResults.subtitle: Patients usually come after trying other treatments without lasting relief.

ProblemReframe.title: Resolving Pain at the Source
ProblemReframe.quote: Temporary relief is easy. Lasting resolution requires identifying the real driver of your chronic pain.
ProblemReframe.headline: Pain Relief Is Temporary. Resolution Requires A Method.
ProblemReframe.problemText: Most treatments focus on masking the immediate pain without addressing why it keeps returning.
ProblemReframe.standardText: Traditional approaches often rely on temporary suppression rather than functional resolution.
ProblemReframe.solutionText: A structured system designed to identify and treat the source, restoring long-term function.
ProblemReframe.benefit1Text: Many patients cycle through short-term fixes that only mask symptoms. We look deeper.
ProblemReframe.benefit2Text: YAPCHANKOR focuses on identifying the real driver of chronic pain through structured assessment.
ProblemReframe.benefit3Text: The goal is structured improvement and long-term resolution, not just surface-level management.

Method.title: The YAPCHANKOR Method
Method.subtitle: A Structured Path to Pain Resolution
Method.step1Text: A comprehensive evaluation to understand your pain history and functional limitations.
Method.step2Text: Pinpointing the exact driver of your chronic pain rather than just treating symptoms.
Method.step3Text: Combining expert manual therapy with our proprietary clinical herbal formulations.
Method.step4Text: A structured plan to restore function and ensure long-term recovery.

ClinicalFormulation.headline: From Heritage Formulation to Modern Clinical Support
ClinicalFormulation.description: Our clinical formulas have been used for decades to support patient recovery. Today, this heritage meets modern science through our PainFix range, bridging tradition and clinical validation.
ClinicalFormulation.heritageText: Rooted in centuries-old injury medicine, refined through clinical practice since 1979.
ClinicalFormulation.modernText: KKM-registered and studied for its anti-inflammatory properties.

ScientificValidation.headline: Scientific Validation Behind Clinical Practice
ScientificValidation.description: Our methods and formulations are backed by scientific research and collaborations with leading institutions.
ScientificValidation.monashText: Laboratory-linked research studying the anti-inflammatory activity of our formulations at the cellular level.
ScientificValidation.markerText: Significant reduction in biologically measurable anti-inflammatory markers observed in cellular studies.
ScientificValidation.recoveryText: Research supports the role of our clinical formulations in supporting natural tissue recovery processes.

Insights.description: We regularly publish practical insights on chronic pain, recovery, and injury management based on decades of treating real patients.
Insights.article1_title: Why Chronic Pain Often Doesn’t Improve
Insights.article1_desc: Many cases persist when underlying structural and inflammatory causes remain unresolved.
Insights.article2_title: The Role of Inflammation in Tissue Healing
Insights.article2_desc: How managing localized inflammation is critical for long-term functional recovery.
Insights.article3_desc: Moving beyond symptomatic relief to address the physical source of discomfort.

FAQ.headline: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.description: Find answers to common questions about the YAPCHANKOR Method and our treatment approach.
FAQ.q1: How long is a typical treatment session?
FAQ.a1: A YAPCHANKOR treatment session typically lasts 60 to 90 minutes. This provides sufficient time for a comprehensive combination of manual therapy, clinical formulations, and functional assessment.
FAQ.q2: What is the cost of a clinical assessment?
FAQ.a2: Clinical assessments at YAPCHANKOR start from a competitive base rate tailored to the complexity of the evaluation. Please contact your nearest branch for the most accurate current pricing.
FAQ.q3: Do I need a referral to visit your clinic?
FAQ.a3: No medical referral is required to visit YAPCHANKOR. You can schedule your clinical assessment directly with any of our four branches without a prior doctor's letter.
FAQ.q4: How many sessions do I need for recovery?
FAQ.a4: Recovery timelines are personalized based on your specific condition and pain history. After your assessment, our clinicians will provide a structured recovery progression plan that outlines the expected number of sessions.

FinalCTA.headline: Start with a Proper Assessment
FinalCTA.description: Persistent or recurring pain often needs structured treatment rather than short-term relief.

Conditions.bookAssessment: Book a certified clinical assessment with our physiotherapy specialists today.
Conditions.whatCausing: What may be causing your condition

back-pain.title: Back Pain Therapy
back-pain.desc: Targeted recovery for persistent back pain using Shaolin-based physiotherapy.
back-pain.intro: Back pain can be caused by various conditions such as muscle or ligament strain, slipped discs, arthritis, skeletal irregularities such as scoliosis, or osteoporosis, which can cause compression fractures. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped thousands of our patients achieve real recovery from many types of back pain. We know very well that back pain can cause you to lose sleep, have difficulty walking or standing, to play sports or even to earn your living. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain has not gone away.
back-pain.step1: When you visit us for back pain therapy, we will first examine your back. This examination will be done by certified physiotherapists. We want to assess your ability to sit, stand, walk and lift your legs. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. Our assessment will help us determine where the pain comes from, how much you the pain has limited your function and movement and whether you have muscle spasms. Back pain can be caused by many factors, and we want to understand how serious your condition is.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have. If you suspect a fracture, please see a doctor first to get an X-ray or MRI done, as we do not have these facilities.
back-pain.step2: Our back pain treatment is a fusion of both Eastern and Western medicine, combining physiotherapy with our scientifically-proven herbal Chinese medicine patches. Our physiotherapists will apply a variety of modalities, such as massage / muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your back muscles and soft tissues. They will also prepare custom made herbal medicine patches to apply on the affected area of your back. These patches are worn by the patient for at least 12 hours, and help with reducing pain and inflammation as well as improving blood circulation and healing rates.As you improve over the course of our therapy, our physiotherapist will teach you exercises to increase your flexibility, strengthen your back and abdominal muscles, and improve your posture. By regularly doing these exercises, you will be able to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our therapy.
back-pain.step3: You will usually start to see your pain reducing within the first 3 sessions. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the initial severity of the back pain, how long or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for therapy. In our nearly 40 year history, we achieve the best long term recoveries for our patients when they complete our recommended therapy regimen.

frozen-shoulder.title: Frozen Shoulder Therapy
frozen-shoulder.desc: Restore mobility and resolve inflammation in restricted shoulder joints.
frozen-shoulder.intro: Frozen shoulder, known also as adhesive capsulitis, is a condition characterized by stiffness and pain in your shoulder joint. The symptoms of a frozen shoulder are pain when you move your shoulder, and limited range of motion of your arm. There are three stages of developing a frozen shoulder, with each stage lasting a few months. For some of you, you may feel more pain at night, and not sleep well as a result. Without any treatment, enduring the stiffness/freezing stage to the frozen stage and then hoping for the thawing/recovery stage can take many months or even years. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many frozen shoulder sufferers achieve real recovery without the use of steroid injections or painful manipulations. We know very well that pain from a frozen shoulder can cause you to lose sleep, to not even be able to put on your clothes yourself, to play sports or use your arm the way you normally used to. It may even affect your ability to earn a living. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and stiffness have not gone away.
frozen-shoulder.step1: During your first visit to us for your frozen shoulder therapy, our physiotherapist will perform a physical exam, to check for pain and to measure your active and passive range of motion. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our therapy, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
frozen-shoulder.step2: Our frozen shoulder therapy is a comprehensive recovery plan for you, combining physiotherapy with our scientifically-proven, pharmaceutical Chinese medicine patches. Our physiotherapists will apply a variety of modalities, such as massage/muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your shoulder joint and the muscles and soft tissues around it. After the physiotherapy session, they will also apply these custom-made herbal medicine patches to wrap around your affected shoulder joint. Our YAPCHANKOR patches should be worn by the patient for at least 12 hours, to reduce pain and inflammation as well as to improve blood circulation and healing rates.As you improve over the course of our therapy, our physiotherapist will teach you a range of motion exercises to increase the mobility of your shoulder. It is particularly important for you to regularly perform these exercises, so that you will be able to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our therapy.
frozen-shoulder.step3: Frozen shoulder is a chronic condition that resulted from many months of gradual stiffening and freezing. Even though we believe our therapy provides faster results than anything else out there, sometimes it may not be fast enough if you are the one suffering from the pain. However, our patients report seeing progress around 7 sessions or around a week of therapy. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the initial stiffness of your frozen shoulder, how old or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for therapy.

shoulder-pain.title: Shoulder Pain Therapy
shoulder-pain.desc: Comprehensive treatment for generalized shoulder injuries and rotator cuff pain.
shoulder-pain.intro: Shoulder pain can affect the joint itself or the surrounding muscles, tissues or ligaments. You will probably experience a reduction in the ability to move and use your arm. In some chronic conditions, you will feel a radiating pain and numbness down your arms. Shoulder pain can be caused by many factors, such as bursitis, frozen shoulder , osteoarthritis, rotator cuff injury, sprains or strains, tendinitis, dislocated shoulder joint or a torn cartilage. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many sufferers seeking shoulder pain therapy in Malaysia to achieve real recovery without the use of steroid injections or painful manipulations. We have helped many different types of shoulder cases and know very well that pain in your shoulder can cause you to lose sleep, to feel distracted from the numbness, tingling or pain, to not even be able to put on your clothes yourself, to play sports or use your arm the way you normally used to. It may even affect your ability to earn a living. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and stiffness has not gone away.
shoulder-pain.step1: During your first visit to us for your shoulder pain therapy, our certified physiotherapist will perform a physical exam, to check for pain and to measure your active and passive range of motion. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
shoulder-pain.step2: Our shoulder pain therapy is the best of both Eastern and Western sports medicine, combining physiotherapy modalities such as such as massage / muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your shoulder joint and the muscles and soft tissues around it, with our time tested and clinically-tested Chinese medicine patches. At your session, our physiotherapists will apply a variety of treatments and then they will prepare our custom made patches to wrap around your affected shoulder joint and muscles after the physiotherapy session. These patches are worn by the patient for at least 12 hours, and help with reducing pain and inflammation as well as improving blood circulation and healing rates.Your shoulder also needs to improve in flexibility and strength, so our physiotherapist will teach you various exercises to improve these aspects. It is very important for you to regularly perform these exercises to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our treatment.
shoulder-pain.step3: Shoulder pain can become chronic if left untreated. Our patients start to feel better within a few days of our treatment. Chronic shoulder pain sufferers usually start feeling results within 7-10 treatment sessions. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the severity and cause of your shoulder pain, how old or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for treatment.

knee-pain.title: Knee Pain Therapy
knee-pain.desc: Effective resolution for joint stiffness and structural knee pain.
knee-pain.intro: Knee pain is a common problem that can affect people of all ages. It can be caused by an injury such as a ruptured ligament or torn cartilage, or by more chronic conditions like arthritis or gout. Common knee conditions are ACL injuries, torn meniscus, patellar tendinitis, knee osteoarthritis and knee bursitis. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped thousands of our patients achieve real recovery from many types of knee pain. We know very well that knee pain can cause you to lose sleep, have difficulty kneeling to perform your prayers, walking or standing, to climb stairs or play sports, and sometimes even to affect your ability to earn a living. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain has not gone away.
knee-pain.step1: When you visit us for knee pain, we will first examine your knees. This examination will be done by certified physiotherapists. We want to assess your ability to sit, stand, walk and lift your legs. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain.Our assessment will help us determine where the pain comes from, how much you the pain has limited your function and movement and whether you have muscle spasms. Knee pain can be caused by many factors, and we want to understand how serious your condition is.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have. If you suspect a ligament tear, please see a doctor first to get an MRI done, as we do not have these facilities.
knee-pain.step2: Our knee pain therapy is a real alternative for patients who are considering more invasive or painful procedures such as injections or surgeries. We use our proven pharmaceutical Chinese medicine together with physiotherapy modalities such as massage / muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your knee and the surrounding muscles and soft tissues. After each session, our patient will have our medicine wrapped around their knees, and they ideally should keep the medicine on for at least 12 hours, to help with healing, pain and inflammation reduction as well as improving blood circulation and healing rates.As your knee improves over the course of our therapy, we will teach you exercises to increase your flexibility, strengthen your knee and leg muscles. By regularly doing these exercises, you will be able to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our treatment.
knee-pain.step3: You will usually start to see your pain reducing within the first 3 sessions. Knee osteoarthritis or ligament tear injuries require a little longer to see results. Sometimes patients only report progress after 7 to 10 sessions. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the initial severity of the knee pain, how long or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for treatment. In our nearly 40 year history, we achieve the best long term recoveries for our patients when they complete our recommended treatment regimen.

neck-pain.title: Neck Pain Therapy
neck-pain.desc: Relieve cervical tension, pinched nerves, and chronic neck stiffness.
neck-pain.intro: Neck pain is a very common issue in today’s desk-bound work environment. With millions of people bending over their computers or smartphones, as well as poor posture over prolonged periods, our neck muscles can get strained. Another common cause of neck pain is osteoarthritis, also known as cervical spondylosis, which happens when the protective cartilage at the end of your bones (vertebrae) wear out. Neck pain can also be caused by nerve compression, which happens when herniated discs or bone spurs in the vertebrae of your neck press on the nerves branching out from the spinal cord. Injury-related neck pain such as whiplash, especially from rear-end auto accidents causing your head to jerk backward and then forward, is another common cause. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many people seeking neck pain treatment in Malaysia, particularly for chronic neck pain such as osteoarthritis or cervical spondylosis, disc degeneration, nerve compression, whiplash injury and stiff necks. We understand that a neck pain can really cause a lot of pain and discomfort, even numbness and “pins and needles” down your arms if your nerves are affected. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and stiffness has not gone away.
neck-pain.step1: During your first visit to us for your neck pain therapy, our physiotherapist will examine your neck for areas of tenderness and to check your range of motion, such as how far you can move your head forward, backward, and side to side. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
neck-pain.step2: Our neck pain treatment consists of both traditional Chinese medicine (herbal patches) and modern medicine, using physiotherapy modalities. Our physiotherapists will teach you posture correction techniques, and will apply a variety of treatments, such as massage / muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your neck and the muscles and soft tissues around it. The difference in YAPCHANKOR recovery plans is in the proven herbal medicine patches we use. At every treatment session, our certified physiotherapists will prepare our herbs into a warm patch to apply on the affected area of your neck after the physiotherapy session. These patches are worn by the patient for at least 12 hours, and help with reducing pain and inflammation as well as improving blood circulation and healing rates.As you improve over the course of our therapy, our physiotherapist will teach you neck-strengthening exercises. It is particularly important for you to regularly perform these exercises, so that you will be able to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our treatment.
neck-pain.step3: Your recovery will depend on what caused your neck pain. If your neck pain is caused by strained muscles, you will probably recover faster than more chronic wear-and-tear conditions such as cervical spondylosis or disc degeneration. Typically acute neck pain patients will experience a reduction of pain and relief within a week while more chronic conditions will take 2 to 3 weeks of treatment.

sprained-ankle.title: Sprained Ankle Therapy
sprained-ankle.desc: Accelerate ligament healing and regain strength after severe ankle sprains.
sprained-ankle.intro: A sprained ankle can happen to anyone at anytime. Most frequently, people sprain their ankle when they fall, jump and land unevenly on their feet or miss a step on an uneven pavement. When this happens, your ankle may be forced into an unnatural position, stretching the ligaments that hold your ankle bones together too much. Ankle sprains can range in severity, from a minor one which may just need you to rest a bit, to one where you may need to have it medically evaluated. Symptoms include bruising, swelling, pain when you step on your foot, restricted range of motion in the foot as well as instability in the ankle. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many people seeking sprained ankle therapy in Malaysia, particularly chronic ankle sprain sufferers get back on their feet. We understand that a sprained ankle can really affect your daily activities, from walking, running and playing sports. Some of our patients are also embarrassed to be seen in public walking in crutches. These are all normal feelings of people who have had their normal lives affected by pain. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and stiffness has not gone away.
sprained-ankle.step1: During your first visit to us for your sprained ankle therapy, our physiotherapist will examine your ankle, foot and lower leg for areas of tenderness and to check your range of motion. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
sprained-ankle.step2: Our sprained ankle therapy for your foot speeds up the healing of your ankle. We use a combination treatment – Chinese medicine (externally-applied patches) and Western physiotherapy. If you are suffering from severe pain, our goal will first be to reduce the pain, swelling and tenderness first. Conventional treatment usually just recommends R.I.C.E. (rest, ice, compression, elevation). We increase the effectiveness of your body’s healing processes with our proven anti-inflammatory Chinese medicine patches.Once the pain has reduced, our physiotherapists will apply a variety of treatments, such as massage / muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your joint and the muscles and soft tissues around it. Every treatment comes with our custom made herbal medicine patches to wrap around your affected foot after the physiotherapy session. These patches are worn by the patient for at least 12 hours, and help with reducing pain and inflammation as well as improving blood circulation and healing rates.As your ankle improves over the course of our therapy, our physiotherapist will teach you strengthening exercises for your lower leg muscles. Once you’ve sprained your ankle, it is easy to sprain it again, so you will need to regularly perform the exercises taught tomaintain and improve the strength in the muscles and soft tissues around your ankle joint.
sprained-ankle.step3: Your recovery will depend on the initial severity of your ankle sprain. New acute sprains will heal faster than chronic ones. However, typically our ankle sprain patients will experience a return to normal function within a few days to a week. It’s important that you are also not putting too much pressure on your ankle during our treatment. Our physiotherapist will advise you on when you can go back to normal activities. Wearing the patches on your foot is not going to impede movement, but you may need to wear sandals or slippers.

wrist-pain.title: Wrist Pain Therapy
wrist-pain.desc: Specialized care for carpal tunnel, repetitive strain, and wrist injuries.
wrist-pain.intro: Wrist pain can be caused by sprains or by long terms repetitive stress injuries such as arthritis or carpal tunnel syndrome. In repetitive stress injuries, if your pain or discomfort is dull or “lenguh” as the Malays say, then it could be due to osteoarthritis. If your pain is of the tingling or pins and needles variety, especially at night, then it could be due to carpal tunnel syndrome. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many people seeking wrist pain therapy in Malaysia to achieve real recoveries that help them to get back to their normal daily life. We understand that severe wrist pain can really affect your life, after all, your hands are so crucial to doing anything. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and stiffness has not gone away.
wrist-pain.step1: During your first visit to us for your wrist pain therapy, our physiotherapist will examine your wrist, arm and hands for areas of tenderness and swelling. We will also test your range of motion and wrist strength. We will ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
wrist-pain.step2: Our wrist pain therapy involves physiotherapy techniques like massage/muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your wrist and the muscles and soft tissues around it. We will then prepare our custom-made and proven herbal medicine patches to wrap around your affected wrist after the physiotherapy session. These patches are worn on your wrist for at least 12 hours, and help with reducing pain and inflammation as well as improving blood circulation and healing rates.As you improve over the course of our treatment, our physiotherapist will teach you exercises for your wrist. You need to perform these wrist exercises regularly to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our treatment.
wrist-pain.step3: Our wrist pain patients usually start seeing results within a few days of treatment. Results at this early stage of our treatment will include reduction in pain levels. Wrist pain treatment at YAPCHANKOR can last from one week to 3 weeks, depending on the severity of the condition. Usually a wrist sprain will take a shorter time to recover than a repetitive stress injury. The key to the faster recovery they see at YAPCHANKOR is our proprietary herbal medicine patches which help expedite the body’s healing rates and to reduce inflammation.Carpal Tunnel Syndrome progress at YAPCHANKOR:

plantar-fasciitis.title: Plantar Fasciitis Treatment
plantar-fasciitis.desc: Resolve heel pain and foot inflammation with targeted functional therapy.
plantar-fasciitis.intro: Plantar fasciitis is an inflammation condition of the tissue at the bottom of your feet, which connects your toes to your heel. It is one of the most common causes of heel pain. The pain is most acute when you step on your feet, particularly in the morning when you first wake up. If you’re a runner, or have a job that requires a lot standing or walking or if you are overweight, then you are more likely to suffer from plantar fasciitis. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many people seeking plantar fasciitis and heel pain treatment in Malaysia to get back on their feet. We understand that heel pain can really make your life miserable, to make you have second thoughts to even walk, which was something you never even thought about before. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and stiffness has not gone away.
plantar-fasciitis.step1: During your first visit to us for your plantar fasciitis therapy, our physiotherapist will examine your foot for areas of tenderness. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
plantar-fasciitis.step2: At YAPCHANKOR, plantar fasciitis treatment is approached by first reducing the inflammation, and then teaching you techniques to strengthen your foot muscles and tendons. Our certified physiotherapists will use massage / muscle release techniques on your plantar fascia and Achilles tendon, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your foot and the muscles and soft tissues around it. At each session, we will also prepare our time-tested medicine patches to wrap around your foot. These patches are worn by the patient for at least 12 hours, and is not uncomfortable to walk with. However, you may need to wear sandals.As the inflammation and pain reduces during our therapy, our physiotherapist will teach you strengthening exercises for your lower leg muscles. Plantar fasciitis happens usually because we are too used to comfortable shoes. So while orthotics may help during the painful inflamed period, you don’t want to depend on them for the rest of your life. That is why you should strengthen your foot with the exercises and try to go barefoot as often as possible.
plantar-fasciitis.step3: Plantar fasciitis is an inflammation condition of the thick band of tissue on the bottom of your foot. Our herbal medicine has strong anti-inflammatory effects to facilitate the reduction of inflammation and to improve healing rates. Wearing the patches on your foot is not going to impede movement, but you may need to wear sandals or slippers. Our patients usually will start seeing results within a few days of their treatment. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the initial severity, how old or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for treatment.

lower-back-pain.title: Lower Back Pain Therapy
lower-back-pain.desc: Deeper intervention for lumbar issues to restore full lower back functionality.
lower-back-pain.intro: Lower back pain affects the area of the back below the rib cage, called the lumbar region. It can be caused by various conditions such as muscle or ligament strain, slipped discs, arthritis, skeletal irregularities such as scoliosis, or osteoporosis, which can cause compression fractures. The pain can range from a dull ache, to a sharp stabbing sensation. Feeling numbness or tingling down your legs is also a common symptom, especially with sciatica, when your sciatic nerve is compressed by inflamed tissues in your lower back. Lower back pain is one of the biggest reasons for missed work. If your pain has persisted for more than 3 months, it is considered chronic. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped thousands of our patients seeking lower back pain treatment in Malaysia to achieve real recovery. We know very well that lower back pain can cause you to lose sleep, have difficulty walking or standing, to play sports, to miss work and affect your earning power. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain has not gone away.
lower-back-pain.step1: When you visit us for lower back pain therapy, we will first examine your affected area. For sciatica treatment, this will include examining your buttocks region. This examination will be done by certified physiotherapists. We want to assess your ability to sit, stand, walk and lift your legs. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain.Our assessment will help us determine where the pain comes from, how much you the pain has limited your function and movement and whether you have muscle spasms. Back pain can be caused by many factors, and we want to understand how serious your condition is.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have. If you suspect a fracture, please see a doctor first to get an X-ray or MRI done, as we do not have these facilities.
lower-back-pain.step2: Our lower back pain therapy does not involve painful manipulation or surgery. Lower back pain is one of the most common conditions we treat, and our proprietary Chinese medicine patches are very effective to reduce the pain and inflammation. We apply these patches after every session on your back. We also use a variety of physiotherapy treatments, such as massage / muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your lower back muscles and soft tissues during each session. For the best results, you should wear our patches for at least 12 hours, as they help facilitate healing, and have been scientifically proven to have powerful anti-inflammatory effects.Our recovery plans are comprehensive – we first resolve the pain, then facilitate the healing process and help you strengthen your back for long-term strength. Our physiotherapists will teach you strengthening and flexibility exercises to improve your core, i.e. your back and abdominal muscles, and to improve your posture. By regularly doing these exercises, you will be able to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our treatment.
lower-back-pain.step3: You will usually start to see your pain reducing within the first 3 sessions. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the initial severity of your lower back pain, how long or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for treatment. In our nearly 40 year history, we achieve the best long term recoveries for our patients when they complete our recommended regimen.

slipped-disc.title: Slipped Disc Therapy
slipped-disc.desc: Non-surgical, structural rehabilitation for herniated and bulging discs.
slipped-disc.intro: Between your vertebrae are rubbery cushions called spinal discs, which have a soft centre and tougher exterior. When the softer centre pushes out through a tear in the tougher exterior, you suffer a slipped disc, also known as a herniated or ruptured disc. In some cases, the slipped disc will impinge on nearby nerves and cause pain, numbness or weakness in your arms or legs. Slipped discs are fairly common, and can be caused by lifting heavy objects with your back muscles instead of your legs. However there is no clear cut reason for this condition. Slipped discs may very well be a function of our modern day sedentary lives which lead to a weakening of our back muscles which make injuries more likely. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many people seeking slipped disc treatment in Malaysia, usually at the lower back (e.g. L4 and L5 vertebrae) or in the neck (cervical) region. Slipped disc sufferers can feel intense pain. If the lower back is affected, then you might feel numbness and weakness in your legs. If the cervical area is affected, then usually there is numbness or weakness in your arms, if the relevant nerves are affected. We understand that a slipped disc problem, like any severe back pain issue, can cause a lot of pain and discomfort. Your normal activities may be significantly impacted, resulting in loss of income even. Slipped disc conditions usually do not require surgery to treat. We know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and has not gone away.
slipped-disc.step1: During your first visit to us for your slipped disc therapy, our certified physiotherapists will examine your spine for areas of tenderness and to check your reflexes and areas of numbness. We may check your walking ability and muscle strength. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
slipped-disc.step2: Our slipped disc therapy is an effective, non-surgical way that uses our powerful and time-tested Chinese medicine patches to reduce inflammation in the affected vertebrae, with modern physiotherapy modalities. Our recovery plan for slipped discs is a comprehensive therapy that reduces pain and inflammation, improves healing rates and increases your spine’s flexibility and strength. Techniques like massage / muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound will be used at various stages of our treatment. At each session, we will freshly prepare our medicine into patches that are applied on your affected back region, and you will need to keep these on for at least 12 hours, ideally.As you feel improvements over the course of the therapy, and your back function returns, we will proceed to teach you back flexibility and strengthening exercises. Exercise is highly important to maintain and improve on the recovery you experience from our treatment.
slipped-disc.step3: Our slipped disc patients usually start seeing results within a few days of therapy. Results at this early stage will include reduction in pain levels. Numbness and weakness will gradually disappear within the 2nd week of therapy and typically our patients will undergo between 2 to 3 weeks of therapy. The key to the faster recovery they see at YAPCHANKOR is our proprietary herbal medicine patches which help expedite the body’s healing rates and to reduce inflammation.

osteoarthritis-knee.title: Osteoarthritis Knee Therapy
osteoarthritis-knee.desc: Manage degenerative joint disease and improve knee lubrication and comfort.
osteoarthritis-knee.intro: Knee osteoarthritis, or degenerative arthritis of the knee, is a type of wear and tear arthritis. Aging is the most common risk factor for the millions of people worldwide who suffer from osteoarthritis knee. Other factors that can also increase your risk are excess weight that put pressure on your knees, gender (women over 55 are more likely than men to develop osteoarthritis of the knee), athletics particularly from long distance running, tennis or football, as well as heredity factors. Symptoms include pain during activity but that reduces with rest, swelling, warmth in the knee joint, stiffness especially in the morning, decrease mobility when you get off from sitting, or when you climb stairs, and hearing creaking or cracking sounds when moving the knee. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many knee osteoarthritis sufferers achieve real long-term recovery without the use of steroid injections or invasive total knee replacement surgery. We know very well that pain from an arthritic knee joint can cause your quality of life to be severely depressed. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work, or are considering a non-invasive alternative before a major surgery.
osteoarthritis-knee.step1: During your first visit to us for your osteoarthritis knee therapy, our physiotherapist will perform a physical exam, to check for pain, swelling and to measure your knee’s active and passive range of motion. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too.If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
osteoarthritis-knee.step2: Our osteoarthritis knee therapy is a non-invasive, painless alternative to surgery. We combine modern physiotherapy with our scientifically-proven herbal Chinese medicine patches. Our physiotherapists will first aim to reduce the pain and inflammation with a variety of techniques such as massage/muscle release techniques, heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound. They will also wrap our proprietary herbal medicine patches around your affected knee after the physiotherapy session. These patches must be worn by you for at least 12 hours, and they help to reduce pain and inflammation as well as to improve blood circulation and healing rates.As you regain function when the pain in your knee reduces, our physiotherapist will progress to facilitating the healing in your knee. Our therapy is always supported by our effective medicine patches, which are the core of our methods. Our physiotherapists will also teach you a strengthening and range of motion exercises to increase the mobility of your knee. With arthritic joints, we need to support the joint’s function with stronger surrounding muscles and tissues, which tend to atrophy when you don’t use your knee regularly.
osteoarthritis-knee.step3: Knee osteoarthritis is a chronic condition that can happen when our bodies age and will typically take about 3 to 4 weeks of treatment sessions with us. Our patients report seeing progress around 7 to 10 sessions or around a week of treatment. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the initial severity of your osteoarthritis, how old or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for treatment.Knee Osteoarthritis patient from Italy tells her recovery story:

osteoarthritis.title: Osteoarthritis Therapy
osteoarthritis.desc: Holistic management of joint wear-and-tear using integrated traditional methods.
osteoarthritis.intro: Osteoarthritis, or wear and tear arthritis, is the most common form of arthritis. Millions of people worldwide suffer from osteoarthritis when the protective cartilage on the ends of their bones wears down over time. Osteoarthritis can happen to any joint in your body but most commonly affects your knees, hips, spine and hands. Symptoms include pain during movement, tenderness when you apply pressure to the joint, stiffness especially when you wake up in the morning or if you sit or lie down too long, loss of flexibility, grating sensation and bone spurs. At YAPCHANKOR, we have helped many osteoarthritis sufferers achieve real long term recovery without the use of steroid injections or invasive surgery. We know very well that pain from an arthritic joint can cause you to lose sleep, to not walk or move normally, or even to affect your ability to earn a living. We also know that you may have tried other treatments that did not work and are feeling frustrated that the pain and stiffness has not gone away.
osteoarthritis.step1: During your first visit to us for your osteoarthritis therapy, our physiotherapist will perform a physical exam, to check for pain, swelling and to measure your joint’s active and passive range of motion. We will also ask you to rate your pain on a scale of zero to 10 and discuss how well you’re functioning with your pain. We focus on real results for our patient, so we make a strong effort to measure your progress throughout our treatment, sometimes with photos and videos too. If you have already seen a doctor or tried any other treatments, it will be helpful if you brought the report or any X-ray, MRIs or CT scans that you may have.
osteoarthritis.step2: Our osteoarthritis therapy for your hip, knee, back, shoulder or hand, is a unique and effective way that can only be found at YAPCHANKOR. This is because while many places offer physiotherapy for osteoarthritis (OA) patients, they do not combine it with our proven Chinese medicine patches. We will apply our patches on your affected joints, and they work by reducing inflammation and pain, and facilitate healing. Our physiotherapists will also use various techniques such as heat, TENs (electrical stimulation), and ultrasound to relax your joint and the muscles and soft tissues around it. Massage will be used when appropriate. After each session, we will apply our medicine patches, which you need to wear for at least 12 hours.When your pain and inflammation reduces, our physiotherapists will teach you a range of motion exercises to increase the mobility of your affected joint. We will also teach you how to strengthen the surrounding muscles and soft tissues like ligaments and tendons to support the arthritic joint. With OA, it is very important that you regularly perform these exercises to maintain and improve the recovery you see from our treatment.
osteoarthritis.step3: Osteoarthritis is a chronic condition that can happen when our bodies age. Even though we believe our therapy provides faster results than anything else out there, sometimes it may not be fast enough if you are the one suffering with the pain. However, our patients report seeing progress around 7 to 10 sessions or around a week of treatment. How quickly you recover will depend on various factors, such as the initial severity of your osteoarthritis, how old or chronic the condition is, whether you are diligent with the exercises we recommend and how often you come back for treatment.Hip Osteoarthritis Testimonial with Before and After Walking Gait Progress:

sciatica.title: Sciatica Treatment
sciatica.desc: Targeted relief for nerve pain radiating from the lower back to the leg.
sciatica.intro: Sciatica is a condition where pain radiates from the lower back down through the buttock and into the leg. It is typically caused by irritation or compression of the sciatic nerve — the longest nerve in the body. The pain can range from a dull ache to a sharp, burning sensation.
sciatica.step1: Your first visit begins with a physical assessment by our certified physiotherapists. We evaluate your lower back, hip movement, nerve involvement, and how the condition is affecting your daily activities. If you have existing X-rays, MRIs or CT scans, bring them along.
sciatica.step2: Your treatment plan combines hands-on physiotherapy, guided rehabilitation, and our clinical herbal formulation. Specific techniques are selected based on your condition and stage of recovery.
sciatica.step3: As your condition improves, we guide you through exercises to strengthen your lower back and hip muscles, improve mobility, and reduce pressure on the sciatic nerve. Consistency with these exercises is essential to maintaining recovery and reducing recurrence.

hip-pain.title: Hip Pain Treatment
hip-pain.desc: Restore hip mobility and reduce pain with targeted functional therapy.
hip-pain.intro: Hip pain can affect how you walk, sit, stand, and move throughout the day. Over time, even simple activities like climbing stairs, getting out of a chair, or turning in bed can become uncomfortable. Unlike knee pain, hip pain is often harder to pinpoint — and many patients find that it persists despite rest or basic treatment.
hip-pain.step1: Your first visit begins with a physical assessment by our certified physiotherapists. We evaluate your hip movement, pain patterns, and how the condition is affecting your daily activities. We also assess related areas such as the lower back and knees, as these often contribute to hip pain. If you have existing X-rays, MRIs or CT scans, bring them along.
hip-pain.step2: Your treatment plan combines hands-on physiotherapy, guided rehabilitation, and our clinical herbal formulation. Specific techniques are selected based on your condition and stage of recovery.
hip-pain.step3: As your condition improves, we guide you through exercises to strengthen the muscles around the hip and improve movement patterns. This is essential for maintaining recovery and reducing recurrence.

elbow-pain.title: Elbow Pain Treatment
elbow-pain.desc: Targeted care for tennis elbow, golfer's elbow and repetitive strain injuries.
elbow-pain.intro: Elbow pain can make simple daily activities difficult — lifting objects, typing, gripping, or even holding a cup can trigger discomfort. Two of the most common conditions are tennis elbow (lateral epicondylitis) — pain on the outer elbow and golfer's elbow (medial epicondylitis) — pain on the inner elbow. These conditions are often caused by repetitive strain rather than a single injury.
elbow-pain.step1: Your first visit begins with a physical assessment by our certified physiotherapists. We examine your elbow, forearm, and wrist, assess pain points, movement limitations, and how the condition is affecting your daily activities. If you have existing imaging, bring it along.
elbow-pain.step2: Your treatment plan combines hands-on physiotherapy, guided rehabilitation, and our clinical herbal formulation. Specific techniques are selected based on your condition and stage of recovery.
elbow-pain.step3: As your condition improves, we guide you through exercises to strengthen the forearm muscles and improve load tolerance. This is essential for preventing recurrence when you return to work or sports activities.

post-surgery-rehab.title: Post-Surgery Rehabilitation
post-surgery-rehab.desc: Recovering from an injury or surgery does not end when the procedure or initial treatment is over.
post-surgery-rehab.intro: Post-surgery recovery is a critical phase where the goal shifts from immediate surgical success to functional restoration. Our <surgery>rehabilitation protocols</surgery> are designed to support tissue healing and rebuild strength safely.

chronic-pain.title: Chronic Pain Treatment
chronic-pain.desc: Many people come to us with the same experience. The pain improves for a while — after rest, medication, massage, or treatment — but then it comes back again.
chronic-pain.intro: When pain persists for months or years, it often involves a combination of structural issues and persistent inflammation. Our <chronic>chronic pain program</chronic> looks beyond surface symptoms to resolve the underlying drivers.

sports-injury.title: Sports Injury Treatment
sports-injury.desc: Sports injuries can happen suddenly — during a game, a run, or even routine exercise. What starts as a minor strain or sprain can develop into a longer-term issue if not properly managed.
sports-injury.intro: Whether it's a <link>sprained ankle</link> or a rotator cuff tear, our <sports>sports injury specialists</sports> help athletes return to their peak performance through integrated clinical care.

hero.label: THE SHAOLIN METHOD
hero.title: OUR METHOD
hero.subtitle: How We Treat Chronic Muscle and Joint Pain
hero.hook: When pain keeps coming back, there is usually a reason. Most chronic muscle and joint pain does not come from a single problem. It comes from a combination of persistent inflammation, restricted movement and weakened supporting structures — none of which has been fully resolved. Treatments that address only one of these factors tend to produce temporary improvement. When the underlying combination remains, the pain returns. Our method is built around resolving all three together, in a structured and integrated way.

physio.title: Hands-On Physiotherapy
physio.text: Targeted manual therapy — including muscle release, heat therapy, TENS and ultrasound — to reduce strain, restore mobility and address the structural contributors to your pain.

rehab.title: Structured Rehabilitation
rehab.text: Progressive exercises designed to rebuild strength and stability around the affected area — so recovery is maintained between sessions and the pain does not return.

herbal.title: Proprietary Herbal Formulation
herbal.text: Shaolin-derived herbal patches applied directly to the affected area after each session and worn for at least 12 hours — working to reduce inflammation and support the body's natural recovery process from the outside in.

integration.title: Why Integration Matters
integration.text2: Physiotherapy restores movement. The herbal formulation reduces the inflammatory environment that would otherwise slow recovery. Rehabilitation ensures the gains hold.
integration.text3: This is not Eastern and Western medicine offered side by side. It is a single treatment approach where every component works in the same direction at the same time.

clinical.title: Aligned With Current Clinical Practice
clinical.text1: Modern clinical guidelines recommend starting with conservative, multidisciplinary treatment before considering injections or surgery.
clinical.text2: This is the same approach we take at YAPCHANKOR — combining physiotherapy, rehabilitation and our clinical formulation in a structured, integrated way.

science.title: The Science Behind the Formula
science.intro: Tradition alone is not enough. This is why our proprietary formulation was evaluated through laboratory research conducted by our research partner, Relivium Sciences (maker of PainFix products). The study examined key inflammatory markers such as TNF-α and IL-6, which are associated with chronic inflammation.
science.finding2: Consistent anti-inflammatory activity across all stages tested
science.finding3: Over 90% cell viability, confirming safety at optimised concentrations
science.conclusion: These findings support the biological activity of the formulation used in our clinical approach.

heritage.title: A Lineage Built on Treating Real Injuries
heritage.text1: The study of medicine and martial arts were inseparable in ancient China. Practitioners needed a deep understanding of anatomy, injury and recovery — developed through real physical training, not theory.
heritage.text2: It was YAPCHANKOR's ancestor, Yap Feng Qi, who trained at the Shaolin Temple in the early 20th century and learned their closely guarded formulas for treating external injuries. From 1916 onwards, his descendants brought these teachings to Malaya — including Yap Shu Shen, who became Grandmaster of the Chin Woo Stadium and spent over 50 years training and treating students and practitioners.
heritage.text3: His son, Yap Chan Kor, established Tabib Yap Chan Kor in 1979. What you see today is the continuation of that lineage — refined through decades of clinical use, supported by modern physiotherapy, and now strengthened by independent scientific research.
heritage.text4: This is not a tradition we inherited and preserved unchanged. It is one we have continued to develop.

product.title: From Clinic to Product
product.text1: The same formulation used in our clinics for over 45 years — across more than 50,000 patients — later inspired the PainFix product range, registered with Malaysia's Ministry of Health (KKM).
product.text2: What began as a clinical treatment is now available in pharmacy-grade topical form. The formulation, the research and the clinical heritage behind it are the same.

measurement.title: Measured, Not Assumed
measurement.text: We track progress through pain levels, range of motion and functional improvement — so that recovery is observable, not assumed.

summary.title: What This Means for You
summary.text1: You are not choosing between traditional and modern care. You are accessing both — in a single integrated treatment approach, delivered by certified physiotherapists and guided by clinical assessment.
summary.text2: For patients who have tried multiple treatments without lasting improvement, this is often the difference.

cta.title: Ready to Find Out if We Can Help?
cta.text: Book a clinical assessment with our physiotherapy specialists today. No referral needed.

Legal.disclaimer: The information on this website is for educational purposes and should not be used as a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.

Testimonials.title: Real Recovery Stories
Testimonials.subtitle: Clinical outcomes and patient experiences from our specialized chronic pain treatment.
Testimonials.heroSubtitle: Proven outcomes across thousands of chronic pain cases using the Shaolin Method since 1979.
Testimonials.noResults: No specific cases found for this category yet.

Science.heroSubtitle: Bridging 40 years of Shaolin clinical heritage with cutting-edge cellular research and laboratory validation.
Science.heritageText: For over 40 years, YAPCHANKOR has utilized time-tested herbal medicine formulas in our clinics with exceptional success. While clinical data suggested high efficacy, we sought technical validation to unlock the biological secrets behind these recovery times.
Science.studyBackground: The purpose of this study was to evaluate the anti-inflammatory characteristics of YAPCHANKOR proprietary herbal extracts using Human Embryonic Kidney (HEK293) cells.
Science.studyMethod: Cells were treated with the herbal formulation and inflammation was induced using Lipopolysaccharide (LPS). Four key inflammatory markers — P-P38MAPK, IL-6, IL-1β, and TNF-α — were studied to measure the immune response.
Science.result1Text: The study confirmed over 90% cell viability, proving the formulation is completely non-toxic at therapeutic concentrations.
Science.result2Text: The extracts significantly inhibited the onset of inflammation by suppressing the expression of key inflammatory markers.
Science.result3Text: Significant anti-inflammatory action was observed as early as 2 hours and sustained for over 24 hours.
Science.step1Text: The proprietary extraction method ensures that active herbal compounds are readily absorbed through the skin.
Science.step2Text: The formulation targets specific inflammatory pathways to reduce swelling and pain at the source.
Science.step3Text: By managing inflammation, the herbal patches create an optimal environment for natural tissue repair.
Science.conclusion: This validation confirms what we have seen in our clinics for decades: YAPCHANKOR herbal patches are a powerful, non-invasive solution for musculoskeletal recovery.
